title: BlackBerry Share Buyback: A Strategic Move to Bolster Shareholder Value
text: BlackBerry has renewed its normal course issuer bid share buyback programme, allowing it to repurchase up to 26,785,714 common shares, equal to about 4.58% of its public float as of April 30, 2026. The renewed NCIB program began on May 12, 2026, and will expire on the earliest of May 11, 2027, completion of the authorized purchases, or a date determined by the company. BlackBerry explicitly linked the programme to capital allocation flexibility and the impact of its equity incentive plan. Using a portion of excess cash for repurchases could provide an attractive return on capital and help offset dilution from shares issued under its equity incentive plan. BlackBerry said it strengthened its balance sheet in fiscal 2026 and expects to generate positive operating cash flow in fiscal 2027. The company said it believes its share price may at times not reflect the underlying value of the business and its prospects. As of April 30, 2026, BlackBerry had repurchased 18,136,158 common shares at a weighted average purchase price of US$3.85 per common share under the previous buyback program.
